mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month declines in industrial production signal persistent manufacturing contraction that reduces corporate earnings expectations and compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In months with month-over-month declines in INDPRO, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50 percent of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate below one percent signals emergency monetary easing that compresses the equity discount rate and stabilizes interbank funding, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS is below 1.0%, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50 percent of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Joint increases in unemployment and declines in retail sales capture a non-linear consumer-labor contraction that depresses aggregate demand more severely than either variable alone, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In months where UNRATE rises month-over-month and RSAFS falls month-over-month, S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 45 percent of occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: During the post-housing-bust financial crisis, month-over-month stabilization or increase in housing starts signals a bottoming of credit-sensitive demand and an eventual equity rebound, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: During financial crisis periods, month-over-month increases in HOUST coincide with negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50 percent of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating job losses captured by month-over-month declines in nonfarm payrolls reduce aggregate income and consumption forecasts, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: On days following a month-over-month decline in PAYEMS, S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 50 percent of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month contraction in real retail sales signals a collapse in consumer demand that precedes negative corporate revenue surprises, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: In months when RSAFS declines month-over-month, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 45 percent of occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter decline in real GDP signals persistent aggregate demand contraction that reduces earnings growth expectations, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: In quarters with negative GDPC1 growth, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50 percent of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month declines in the CPI signal deflationary pressure that raises real debt burdens and depresses nominal corporate pricing power, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When CPIAUCSL falls month-over-month, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50 percent of instances.
